last_item: Mode-S convergence pass (backlog actionable-empty — only NEEDS-BACKEND items + low/watch copy nits remain). Ran the 5-agent parallel discovery panel, same coverage as iters 15–25, HIGH bar + full shipped/rejected digest: (1) Dashboard+Query, (2) Roles+Backups+BackupStorageForm, (3) Alerts+Migrate, (4) Settings+DatabaseTuning+Pooler+Login, (5) nav/IA+first-run+cross-view consistency.
last_result: ALL FIVE VIEWS CONVERGED. Three agents hard-converged (Dashboard+Query; Alerts+Migrate; Settings+Tuning+Pooler+Login — the last correctly self-rejected the low/watch lockout-duration copy nit on restraint, noting only that the backend does send locked_until, which keeps it a parked low item). Two agents floated ONE candidate each, and BOTH collapsed as FALSE PREMISES on code inspection (self-rejected, no panel, iter-7/13 precedent): (1) Roles/Backups agent — gate the RestoreModal 'Restore now' button on a non-empty datetime when PITR mode is selected, self-rated HIGH/S on a 'silently restores latest instead of the requested point-in-time' framing. FALSE: the datetime Input (Backups.tsx:885) has `required` and the button (:835) is a real type=submit form=restore-form control, so HTML5 constraint validation BLOCKS submission on an empty rendered required field (native prompt; Enter-in-confirm submits the form too → same validation; no formNoValidate). When mode is 'latest' the field isn't rendered (:877) so target=null/latest is the intended path. The agent FOUND `required` works and then rationalized 'HTML5 is insufficient UX'; stripped of the false silent-wrong-action framing it's just 'duplicate a native required-gate with a disabled button' — a sweeping prefer-disabled-over-required preference, restraint-negative. (2) nav/IA agent — add tooltip={item.label} to each SidebarMenuButton for the collapsed state, self-rated High/S, 'affects every mobile user.' FALSE: <Sidebar> uses no collapsible prop → default 'offcanvas' (ui/sidebar.tsx:154), a collapsed sidebar slides fully off-screen (no icon rail, :221 w-0), and mobile renders a Sheet with FULL labels (:181). The sidebar tooltip is gated hidden={state!=='collapsed'||isMobile} (:533) → zero visible effect anywhere in this app's mode; 'every mobile user' is flatly wrong. No code shipped, docs-only commit, no hard gates needed. Per the contract (backlog actionable-empty AND fresh discovery surfaced no high/med item): SECOND stable_streak increment 1 -> 2. One more clean pass -> write COMPLETE.md and stop.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
